rulings.Case Title: K.C. PATEL AND SHANTABEN K. PATEL VS. NARENDRA SINGH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 13, 2025 line 2. CROSS DEFENDANT TEMESGHEN GEBREMICHAEL MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is DENIED. According to the Notice of Motion this motion for judgment on the pleadings is made solely pursuant to CCP 438. This statutory motion for judgment on the pleadings is untimely. The Notice of Motion to does not list non-statutory/common law as grounds for the motion. Additionally, whether the management agreement is misclassifying the cross-defendant as in independent contractor is an issue of fact. The Court also notes that XD does not address paragraph "29 Legal Construction" of the Management Agreement, while only pointing to 2 out of 33 clauses of the contract as "illegal." =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CLARENCE VINCENT TOWNSEND JR. ET AL VS. MELVIN WASHINGTON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 13, 2025 line 4. DEFENDANT MELVIN, WASHINGTON, MASUMI WASHINGTON MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. The motion is DENIED as to the first cause of action and only due to the allegation regarding prescriptive easement over a concrete walkway. As stated in Hansen "[s]uch nonexclusive easements [road build across the property of another] do not create the same problem that arises when a purported easement prevents the servient tenement landowner from using the land for any "practical purpose," due to the servient tenement's ability to use the same road for driving. Here, the concrete walkway could be used for walking by both the dominant and servient tenement. Hansen v. Sandridge Partners, L.P. (2018) 22 Cal.App.5th 1020. It is a factual issue of whether it is something that may happen and is not appropriate for a determination on a demurrer. The Court notes that the rest of the claimed prescriptive rights over the entirety of the Defendant's property (e.g. gardening, social gatherings) or over specific parts of the property that appear to be available for use only to the dominant estate (e.g. shed, flower bed) fall squarely within Harrison decision. Harrison v. Welch (2004) 116 Cal. App. 4th 1084. Motion is GRANTED with leave to amend as to the cause of action for equitable easement for Plaintiff to allege facts in support of each element of this cause of action, including innocence of the encroacher. Plaintiff shall be mindful of the sham pleading doctrine. Plaintiffs did not allege prescriptive easement and equitable easement in the alternative in the Complaint and appear to claim an equitable easement arising out of the prescriptive easement without citing any authority for this proposition in the opposition to the demurrer. Motion is GRANTED without leave to amend as to the cause of action for declaratory relief as it is wholly derivative of the other causes of action. Plaintiff provided no opposition to this argument raised in the moving papers. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
